title: spec-graph v1 desired-state semantic metamodel
description: Typed node/edge desired-state graph (the S in N(S)=N(P(E(C),S))). Open versioned kind registry spanning architecture AND code-level semantics. Nodes carry two ids: logical_id (idmodel, cross-revision identity, narrow) and content_digest (canon semantic-content over all formal fields, wide). Edges reference nodes by name; endpoints are typed but node-only in v1. rationale/notes/source_position are a non-hashed envelope (canon content/envelope split).

graph_id.description: Author label for the graph. ENVELOPE-ONLY: never enters the graph digest.

rationale.description: Non-hashed prose envelope for the whole graph.

notes.description: Non-hashed notes envelope for the whole graph.

level.description: Decomposition level. Maps to idmodel file_role.

node_kind.description: Open versioned kind registry (v1 closed-world set). Adding a kind = minor registry bump.

name.description: Author-supplied dotted stable identifier (maps to idmodel qualified_symbol). Required on every node.

tags.description: Schema-declared ORDER-INSENSITIVE string set (sorted during Normalize, per canon section 4).

permitted_freedom.description: Phase-3a parity hook (SEMANTICS.md section 3): order-insensitive token set declaring what realized code may vary below the frontier without a soundness violation. FORMAL/hashed; sorted in Normalize like tags. Tokens MUST be drawn from the vocabulary spec-parity/permitted-freedom-vocab.json (the single source of truth; v1 = open_interface, order_insensitive_enum); an unknown token is a validation error in v1 (additive across versions). The same vocabulary drives the spec-engine soundness matcher (dispatch on rule_kind).

prohibition_domain.description: Closed-world domain descriptor for a prohibition (Phase-3a parity hook, SEMANTICS.md section 4). Bounds the fact set the prohibition is decided over; without it a prohibition is `unsupported`. The optional scan-predicate fields below pin the DECIDABLE assertion for a domain type (SEMANTICS section 4.1/4.4): a domain without its predicate has no scanner and stays `prohibition_domain_unscanned` -> `unsupported`. FORMAL/hashed — the set sub-fields (edge_types/forbidden_call_targets/kinds) are order-insensitive token sets sorted during Normalize; `type`/`allowed_rule` are hashed verbatim.

type.description: v1 domain type. `external` always yields `unknown` (extractor does not model it).

kinds.description: node_kind_set: the realized node kind(s) the domain ranges over (SEMANTICS section 4.1). ORDER-INSENSITIVE token set: sorted during Normalize (like tags), so author order never enters the graph digest.

allowed_rule.description: node_kind_set: the rule deciding which in-domain nodes are PERMITTED (vs a violation), e.g. `corresponds_to_declared_desired_interface`.

edge_types.description: edge_set: the realized edge type(s) the domain ranges over, e.g. ["calls"] (SEMANTICS section 4.4). ORDER-INSENSITIVE token set: sorted during Normalize (like tags), so author order never enters the graph digest.

forbidden_call_targets.description: edge_set: the forbidden target tokens (callee identifiers) — an edge in the domain whose target token equals one of these is a violation. Exact token equality, NOT substring (SEMANTICS section 4.4). ORDER-INSENSITIVE token set: sorted during Normalize (like tags), so author order never enters the graph digest. A bare edge_set with no forbidden_call_targets has no decidable assertion -> `prohibition_domain_unscanned` -> `unsupported`.

signature.description: Structured signature (idmodel section 4). Param NAMES dropped during normalization; types canonicalized.

node_provenance.description: Draft-S provenance marker (IF-0-BOOT-1, brownfield freeze). ENVELOPE-ONLY: NON-HASHED — does NOT enter the graph digest OR the node logical_id (a node with vs without provenance is byte-identical in both ids), exactly like rationale/notes. Records how a bootstrapped node originated: code_derived (E(C) scaffold), doc_derived (docs intent), human_authored (ratified intent). A node WITHOUT a provenance block is treated as human_authored (the pre-brownfield default — a hand-authored S is an authority).

origin.description: How this draft node originated. Only human_authored (and not unratified) is an authority (anti-vacuity rule).

boundary_id.description: Optional: the realized E(C) boundary this code-derived node was scaffolded from.

clause_id.description: Optional: the docs clause this doc-derived intent node was proposed from.

source_position.description: DSL provenance. ENVELOPE-ONLY: excluded from the graph digest AND from round-trip equality (re-derived on parse).

frontier.description: Phase-3a parity hook (SEMANTICS.md section 1.2.2): node is in-scope for a parity run. Default false. FORMAL/hashed.

members.description: type: ordered member list (enum variants / record fields). FORMAL/hashed as an ORDERED list (desired-side declaration order is content). The `order_insensitive_enum` permitted_freedom token frees only the REALIZED-side comparison order, not the desired-side hashed order.

expression.description: invariant: the property that must hold.

payload_ref.description: event: name of the payload type node.

budget.description: performance: the budget, as a string (no floats per canon).

observable.description: prohibition: can an extractor observe a violation? false => Phase-3 unknown, never silent pass.

unratified.description: Draft-S marker (IF-0-BOOT-1): the node is still an UNRATIFIED bootstrap draft (a human has not asserted its intent). TRUE-OR-ABSENT: omit it for a ratified node — an explicit `false` is rejected (it would break the DSL round-trip, since the truthiness-based envelope render drops a falsy field while Normalize preserves it). ENVELOPE-ONLY: NON-HASHED — does not enter the graph digest OR logical_id. Absent = ratified/not-a-draft. A never-ratified, all-code-derived S is NOT an authority (anti-vacuity rule).

endpoint_kind.description: v1: node-only. Typed so a future subgraph/code_symbol endpoint kind is additive. (Resolved open question: edges do NOT target subgraphs.)
